name: Software Engineering, Analysis, and Design

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: A strong software-engineering spine is present that maps cleanly onto the certificate's name: requirements (IS 676), analysis and design (IS 663), design/production methodology (CS 673), architecture (CS 685), testing and QA (CS 684), and project management (CS 683), covering the full SDLC. The sampled inventory (68 referenced, 40 shown) also carries considerable noise unrelated to SE (emergency-management IS 612/613/614/616, co-op IS 590-593, and Master's-project shells IS 700/700B/700C), which blurs the focus.
dimensions.suggestion: Curate the certificate to the SE core (CS 673, CS 684, CS 685, IS 663, IS 676) plus a short design/architecture elective list, and drop co-op and emergency-management courses from the credential's inventory.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: The graph is nearly clean: deepestPrereqChain=2 and only one dangling reference (missingCourseCount=1, IS 790A). Sensible layering exists (IS 663/CS 673 -> IS 684 business-process innovation; IS 601 -> IS 631/IS 634).
dimensions.suggestion: Resolve the single dangling reference to IS 790A (correct the code or link the course) so the prerequisite graph is fully consistent.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No program-level learning outcomes are provided, so the design, analysis, and engineering competencies the certificate certifies are unstated.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish 3-5 outcomes covering requirements engineering, software design/architecture, and quality assurance.

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: statedTotalCredits is 3.0, which is implausible for a multi-course certificate and is almost certainly a parsing artifact (a single course's credits), while creditReconcileDelta is null; this makes the credential's true size opaque and internally inconsistent with its 40-course inventory.
dimensions.suggestion: Correct the stated total to the real requirement (typically ~12 credits / 4 courses) and list exactly which courses satisfy it.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: Course descriptions are strong and specific (IS 663 names agile/lean and process models; CS 685 explains module structure and architecture principles), but the erroneous 3-credit total, absence of a plan (requirementGroups=0), and the sprawling, partly off-topic course list undermine the page's clarity.
dimensions.suggestion: Rebuild the entry around a labeled plan of study with corrected credits and only the SE-relevant courses shown.

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: Coverage reflects modern SE practice: agile/lean analysis (IS 663), architecture (CS 685), testing/QA (CS 684), requirements (IS 676), web services and middleware (IS 690), enterprise architecture (IS 685), and cloud (CS 643). The gaps are DevOps/CI-CD, microservices, and secure development.
dimensions.suggestion: Add a DevOps/CI-CD course and integrate secure-coding and microservices/cloud-native design so graduates match current engineering workflows.

descriptionQuality.justification: Descriptions are detailed and practice-oriented (CS 684 on fault reduction and software quality; IS 676 on requirements as the start of the SDLC; IS 685 on enterprise-architecture integration); none are placeholder, though the project-shell entries (IS 700 series) are necessarily administrative.

prerequisiteClarity.justification: Most prerequisites are clear, but one dangling reference (IS 790A) exists and several fields fold description text into the prerequisite (IS 631, IS 665, IS 690), leaving some entry requirements ambiguous.

missingTopics.rationale: CS 683 covers project management and CS 685 architecture, but no course addresses build/release automation and delivery pipelines central to modern software teams.
CS 684 is quality-focused, not security-focused; there is no coverage of threat modeling or secure coding that design certificates now expect.
CS 685's architecture treatment is general; explicit microservice, API, and container patterns (extending CS 643 and IS 690) are absent.

missingTopics.topic: Software maintenance, refactoring, and technical debt
missingTopics.rationale: The SDLC coverage stops at production; sustaining, evolving, and refactoring large systems is not represented.
IS 663 introduces agile/lean, but scaled frameworks and product-team practices are not deepened anywhere in the core.

emergingTrends.rationale: LLM coding assistants are reshaping design and QA workflows that CS 673/CS 684 could incorporate.
Integrating security into CI/CD would unite CS 684 (QA) with secure-development practice increasingly demanded by employers.
Extending CS 685 and IS 690 toward cloud-native decomposition matches dominant industry architecture.
As software increasingly embeds ML, the design/testing core (CS 673/CS 684) should address model lifecycle and data pipelines.
Internal developer platforms and low-code tooling are changing how analysis/design translate to delivery, a natural extension of IS 663/IS 685.

topSuggestions: Correct the implausible 3-credit stated total and publish the true ~12-credit required SE core (CS 673, CS 684, CS 685, IS 663, IS 676).
Trim unrelated courses (emergency-management IS 612-616, co-op IS 590-593) from the certificate's inventory to restore focus.
Add DevOps/CI-CD and secure-development coverage to align the design/analysis emphasis with current engineering practice.
Resolve the dangling IS 790A reference and clean prerequisite fields that contain description text.
Publish 3-5 program-level learning outcomes for requirements, design/architecture, and QA.
